keyDetails.Requests: R1: Programme-wide limit sweep (47 cards, 1 SAS flag) • R2: New cardholder onboarding (8 joiners) • R3: Virtual card settlement query

sidebarArtifacts.label: DLA Consulting New Joiners — Google Sheet (es → en)

logs.title: Inbound email received from Rachel Thornton (PA) at DLA Piper — voice note attached
reasoning: Inbound email received from rachel.thornton@dlapiper.com at 09:01:04 GMT to hsbc.commercialcards.uk@hsbc.com.
Sender domain verified as dlapiper.com — matched to active programme DLA-UK-CC-2024. SM assigned: David Mensah.
Email contained a 47-second voice note attachment (DLA_Voice_Note_Spanish.mp3). Voice note transcribed in parallel using speech-to-text. Language detected: Spanish.

Three distinct requests extracted from email body and voice note: R1 — programme-wide limit sweep for UK Marketing sub-programme (47 cards, £3,000 → £5,000); R2 — new cardholder onboarding for 8 consulting joiners (Spanish Google Sheet attached); R3 — virtual card payment query for supplier SUP-0441.
All three requests confirmed within PA authority scope under POL-DLA-UK-2024. Three parallel workstreams initialised at 09:01:09 GMT.

data.subject: Three things — DLA programme
data.body:
Hi HSBC,

Three things I need sorting today — I'll be quick.

1. UK Marketing sub-programme (47 cards): our Q2 push starts Monday and the team needs limits lifted from £3,000 to £5,000 across the board. Total programme headroom should cover it — please confirm and apply.

2. Eight new joiners in Consulting — details in the attached Google Sheet (sorry, our Madrid office sent it in Spanish). Standard limit for each, please.

3. One of our suppliers (SUP-0441) says they haven't received payment against PO #PO-2026-0298. The virtual card should have settled — can you confirm and send me the reference?

Thanks as always,
Rachel

--
Rachel Thornton
Global Programme Administrator | DLA Piper LLP
rachel.thornton@dlapiper.com | +44 20 7153 7000 ext. 3241

logs.title: Intent parsed — 3 distinct requests identified, R1 dispatched first
reasoning: NLP classification applied to email body and voice note transcript.
R1 classified: programme-wide limit amendment — 47 cards, UK Marketing sub-programme, £3,000 → £5,000. Requires programme credit line check before card-level changes.
R2 classified: new cardholder onboarding — 8 joiners, Consulting sub-programme. Spanish-language attachment detected; extraction queued.
R3 classified: virtual card payment query — supplier SUP-0441, PO #PO-2026-0298. Requires Taulia portal + Auth Platform check.
R1 dispatched to programme sweep workstream. R2 and R3 queued in parallel.

logs.title: [R1] Programme sweep initiated — UK Marketing sub-programme, 47 cards
reasoning: R1 workstream initiated. Target: all 47 cards under DLA-UK-CC-2024 UK Marketing sub-programme.
MiVision queried at programme level for credit line headroom check.
Card-level eligibility sweep queued: Auth Platform + SAS check for all 47 cards in parallel.

logs.title: [R1] Programme credit line validated — headroom confirmed
reasoning: Pace queried MiVision at programme level for DLA-UK-CC-2024.
Current total programme credit limit: £2,350,000. Current aggregate card utilisation: £847,200 (36%).
UK Marketing sub-programme: 47 active cards, aggregate current limits £141,000. Requested uplift: 47 × £2,000 = £94,000 additional exposure.
Post-uplift aggregate for UK Marketing: £235,000. Total programme exposure post-change: £941,200 — within the programme ceiling with £1,408,800 remaining headroom.
Programme credit line check passed. Proceeding to card-level eligibility sweep.

logs.title: [R1] SAS sweep complete — 1 flag raised on card ••••3847
reasoning: Pace ran all 47 cards through Auth Platform and SAS in parallel batch mode.
46 cards returned clean: no open alerts, no outstanding disputes, no compliance holds.

Card ••••3847 (cardholder: James Osei, Senior Account Director) returned an active SAS flag: alert SAL-2026-0228-3847, raised 11 days ago for suspicious transaction pattern. Alert status: Open. No disposition recorded. Escalated to Fraud Ops 8 days ago with no resolution.
Limit increase cannot be auto-processed for card ••••3847 under programme policy POL-DLA-UK-2024 Section 9.4 — open fraud alert blocks limit amendments.
46 clean cards queued for Inlogik bulk amendment. Card ••••3847 quarantined for HITL decision.

logs.title: [R1] Inlogik bulk amendment submitted — 46 clean cards updated £3,000 → £5,000
reasoning: Pace navigated to Inlogik CMP, selected UK Marketing sub-programme, filtered to the 46 flagged-clean cards.
Bulk limit amendment applied: £3,000 → £5,000 for all 46 cards simultaneously. Amendment reference: BLK-2026-0322-DLA-046.
MiVision confirmation screenshot captured — updated limits visible on all 46 card profiles.
Card ••••3847 excluded from this amendment batch and held pending HITL decision.
Total cards updated: 46. Total additional programme exposure applied: £92,000.

logs.title: [R1] HITL required — James Osei ••••3847 flagged, open SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847
reasoning: Card ••••3847 blocked from auto-amendment due to active, unresolved SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847.
Alert raised: 28 February 2026. Escalated to Fraud Ops: 14 March 2026. Current status: Open. No disposition recorded.
Three options presented to SM David Mensah for decision on card ••••3847.

hitlOptions.label: Option A
hitlOptions.description: Hold limit increase for card ••••3847. Notify PA that 46/47 cards updated, one under review. No further escalation.
hitlOptions.risk: PA may chase. Fraud alert continues to sit unactioned.

hitlOptions.label: Option B — Recommended
hitlOptions.description: Hold limit increase, escalate SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847 to Fraud Ops for urgent disposition. Send PA holding response: 46/47 complete, one card under active review.
hitlOptions.risk: Proactive. Forces Fraud Ops to close the loop. PA gets transparency without investigative detail.

hitlOptions.label: Option C
hitlOptions.description: Process limit increase for all 47 cards including ••••3847 and flag internally.
hitlOptions.risk: Increases exposure on a card with an unresolved fraud flag. Compliance risk.

logs.title: [R2] Google Sheet attachment detected — language identified: Spanish
reasoning: Attachment DLA_Consulting_New_Joiners_March2026.xlsx extracted from email.
Language detection: Spanish (es). Sheet contains 8 rows of new joiner data.
Columns detected: Nombre completo, Correo electrónico, DNI/Pasaporte, Fecha de inicio, Departamento, Oficina.
Translation pipeline initiated. Data being extracted and mapped to HSBC cardholder onboarding schema.

artifacts.label: DLA Consulting New Joiners — Google Sheet (es → en)

logs.title: [R2] Document extraction complete — 8 cardholder records structured in English
reasoning: All 8 rows extracted and translated from Spanish to English.
Cardholder records validated: names, emails, IDs, start dates, departments, office locations all present.
Programme defaults applied: DLA Piper Consulting onboarding template loaded — standard limit £4,000 per card, cost centre DLA-CONS-2024.
No data quality issues. 8 records queued for Inlogik bulk submission.

logs.title: [R2] Programme defaults applied — DLA Piper onboarding template loaded
reasoning: DLA Piper Consulting onboarding template loaded from programme configuration.
Defaults applied: card limit £4,000 (standard new joiner), cost centre DLA-CONS-2024, billing cycle monthly, statement currency GBP.
CRM hierarchy check passed: all 8 applicants verified as new DLA Piper employees. No duplicates detected.
Batch of 8 card applications prepared for Inlogik submission.

logs.title: [R2] Bulk submission to Inlogik — 8 card applications submitted
reasoning: Pace navigated to Inlogik CMP and submitted 8 new cardholder applications in batch.
Bank-acceptance gate confirmed: PA Rachel Thornton cannot self-serve new card issuance in MiVision — HSBC submission required.
All 8 applications submitted under programme DLA-UK-CC-2024, Consulting sub-programme, £4,000 limit each.
Inlogik batch reference: ISS-2026-0322-DLA-008. Expected card issuance: 3–5 business days.

logs.title: [R2] Confirmation email drafted — 8 new joiner cards in progress
reasoning: Draft confirmation email prepared covering R2 outcome.
Email confirms 8 applications submitted, Inlogik batch reference ISS-2026-0322-DLA-008, expected issuance 3–5 business days.
Included in consolidated reply — will be sent once SM approves R1 HITL decision.

logs.title: [R3] Voice note transcribed — virtual card payment query identified
reasoning: Voice note (DLA_Voice_Note_Spanish.mp3) transcribed from Spanish to English.
Content: Rachel queries why supplier SUP-0441 has not received payment against PO #PO-2026-0298.
Query type classified: virtual card settlement status check.
Virtual card lookup initiated: searching Auth Platform for card linked to PO #PO-2026-0298.

logs.title: [R3] Query intent: virtual card payment status — supplier SUP-0441, PO #PO-2026-0298
reasoning: Pace navigated to SAP Taulia portal for DLA Piper account.
PO #PO-2026-0298 located: supplier SUP-0441, invoice amount £12,840.00, due date 18 March 2026.
Virtual card VC-2026-0298-HSBC identified as the payment instrument for this PO.
Auth Platform queried for virtual card authorisation and settlement status.

logs.title: [R3] Virtual card located — VC-2026-0298-HSBC, payment authorised and settled
reasoning: Virtual card VC-2026-0298-HSBC confirmed: authorisation approved 17 March 2026 at 14:22 GMT. Amount: £12,840.00.
Settlement confirmed: 18 March 2026. Settlement reference: STL-2026-0318-VC0298.
Supplier-side delay likely: funds settled to acquiring bank but may take 1–2 business days to reflect in supplier's account.
Taulia payment record captured. Confirmation email drafted for PA with settlement reference and advice to supplier.

logs.title: [R3] Confirmation email drafted — settlement reference provided to PA
reasoning: Draft R3 response prepared: settlement reference STL-2026-0318-VC0298, funds cleared 18 March, recommendation for supplier to check with acquiring bank.
Included in consolidated reply — queued pending SM email send action on R1 HITL.

logs.title: [R1] HITL resolved — Option B selected, ••••3847 escalated to Fraud Ops
reasoning: SM David Mensah selected Option B: hold limit increase for card ••••3847, escalate SAS alert SAL-2026-0228-3847 to Fraud Ops for urgent disposition.
Fraud Ops escalation logged. Urgent disposition request submitted referencing open alert and PA limit request as catalyst.
Card ••••3847 limit remains at £3,000 pending Fraud Ops resolution.
PA holding response drafted: 46/47 cards updated, one card under active review.

logs.title: All 3 requests complete — consolidated reply sent to Rachel Thornton
reasoning: R1: 46/47 cards updated £3,000 → £5,000. Card ••••3847 held; Fraud Ops alerted for urgent resolution.
R2: 8 new joiner cards submitted to Inlogik. Batch reference ISS-2026-0322-DLA-008. ETA 3–5 business days.
R3: Virtual card VC-2026-0298-HSBC payment confirmed settled 18 March. Settlement reference STL-2026-0318-VC0298 shared with PA.
Consolidated reply sent to rachel.thornton@dlapiper.com covering all three requests.
CRM case logged with full audit trail.
